title: PHI leaks through logs, URLs, and error messages
description: Patient data commonly escapes not through the database but through debug logs, URL query strings, stack traces, analytics, and third-party scripts.
body: Use when reviewing where PHI might unintentionally surface. Even with a secured datastore, PHI leaks via request logs, identifiers in URLs (which land in browser history and proxy logs), verbose error/stack traces, third-party analytics or telemetry, and screenshots. Do not assume securing the database secures the data. Actions: keep PHI out of URLs and logs, redact it from errors and telemetry, and audit third-party scripts on PHI-bearing pages. Validation signals include log/trace scans showing no PHI and a check that identifiers are not passed in URLs. Risk boundary: advisory only; a confirmed PHI leak into logs or a third party is a reportable disclosure to escalate to a human, not a quiet log scrub.
